steps.title: List the zero-producing factors
steps.content:
The graph has an $x$-intercept whenever $y=0$. The factors are
$$3(x-k)(x+k)(x+r)(x-k)$$
The repeated factor $(x-k)$ gives the same intercept twice, not two distinct intercepts.

steps.title: Count the distinct intercepts
steps.content:
The distinct roots are
$$x=k,\quad x=-k,\quad x=-r$$
Because $k$ and $r$ are unique positive constants, these are $3$ different $x$-values. The answer is B.
